mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels directly increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and compressing forward discounted cash flows, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals impending economic deceleration, triggering a flight to quality that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates weakening consumer spending and housing market contraction, which jointly suppresses corporate revenue growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a sustained increase in INDPRO reflects resilient industrial output that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive forward returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding PAYEMS levels signal robust employment growth that supports household consumption and corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns or produces a Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, compressing profit margins and equity valuations, which forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >50% of periods where FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0%, invalidating the cost-of-capital transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDPC1 and falling INDPRO reveals demand outpacing supply capacity, creating inflationary bottlenecks that force central bank tightening and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-INDPRO divergence fails to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, declining CPIAUCSL removes inflation constraints on monetary policy, allowing equity risk premiums to compress and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive forward returns during recovery phases, with a mean return difference statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
